trivia_bullets.text: Commander Legends (CMR), released November 20, 2020, was the first Magic set designed explicitly as a draftable Commander format — players drafted with 20-card packs taking two cards per pick, then built 60-card singleton-optional Commander decks. Fertilid is a reprint inclusion in that environment, its ramp utility making it a reliable green draft pick for mana-hungry Commander shells. [Scryfall; Hipsters of the Coast, 'A First Look at Commander Legends', 2020]

Fertilid originated in Morningtide (February 2008), the follow-up small set to Lorwyn, where the counter-as-resource theme ran through the set's tribal designs. It has been reprinted 14 times across Planechase, Archenemy, Commander 2011, Duel Decks: Nissa vs. Ob Nixilis, Battlebond, Ikoria, Jumpstart, Zendikar Rising Commander, Commander Legends, Neon Dynasty Commander, Jumpstart 2022, and March of the Machine Commander — making it one of the most broadly distributed common ramp creatures in the game. [Scryfall prints search]

On EDHREC, Fertilid holds an edhrec_rank of 2686 and appears in approximately 1.32% of Commander decks — roughly 54,000 decks surveyed. Its appeal is the 'target player' clause: unlike most land-ramp, Fertilid can fetch for any player, making it useful in group-hug and politics strategies as well as straightforward ramp. [Scryfall edhrec_rank field; EDHREC card page]

The Commander Legends printing was illustrated by Wayne Reynolds, a British fantasy illustrator whose MTG originals command strong auction prices — his Feather, the Redeemed original sold for $6,050 (2019) and his Bruenor Battlehammer original sold for $26,000 (2021). Fertilid is a common, but the Reynolds credit connects it to a body of high-value original work. [Hipsters of the Coast, 'Painting for Bruenor Battlehammer by Wayne Reynolds Sells for $26,000', 2021; 'Art for Feather, the Redeemed by Wayne Reynolds Sells for $6,050', 2019]
